[Gaming] Swag, Fight and meet the devs! Mixed format tournament.
Riot's 2v2 tag fighter brings League of Legends champions to the fighting game arena! Fresh mechanics, familiar faces, and new rivalries. Top 3 win prizes!

events.title: Name that Anime Tune 2.0
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] How well do you REALLY know your anime music? Compete against the room to identify the songs of anime, but with a twist: playback will start at a random point in each track! This is the spectacular trivia experience of Name That Anime Tune 2.0 but in a whole new light!

events.title: ElfQuest: An American Manga
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] For almost 50 years, ElfQuest has straddled the line of what defines an American comic and what defines manga. A look at the series, and why it could be considered an American manga.

events.title: Spoilers Ahead: Let's ruin everything!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Some people just don’t care about spoilers. Sometimes there’s a story you’re interested in, but just don’t have time to sit down and watch or play the whole thing. Or maybe you just want to kill some time listening to someone talk about stories they really like! Whatever you come in for, you’ll have a good time! I’ve got lots of things to spoil for you, including Silent Hill F, and one of Kamen Rider's most infamous moments! There are some things that I'll only talk about in this room!

events.title: Iconic Kpop Random Play Dance
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you a fan of Kpop? Want to show off you moves! Then come join us for our iconic KPOP RPD!!! Songs from 2005 to 2026!!!

events.title: Flower Arranging with A Voice Actor (Kelsey Cruz)
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] From Florist to Voice Actress - she does it all! Kelsey Cruz used to work at a flower shop as a florist and would make arrangements for weddings, funerals, birthdays, and everything inbetween! Join as she takes you through how to arrange your own flowers at home while fielding your questions about flowers, anime, voice acting, etc!

events.title: AMV Contest Seating
events.panelDescription: [Main Stage] Seating for the AMV Contest

events.title: Strange Anime For Strange People
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you a weirdo who likes to explore the weird, offputting side of whatever media you consume? Well, this is the panel for you! Surreal humor, mind-bending visuals, outrageous characters, and more await as we highlight the anime and creators that dare to be different.

events.title: The Gojo Effect
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you ever wondered why people are obsessed with Satoru Gojo from the series Jujutsu Kaisen? Do you, yourself, love him? Do you hate him and want to know what it is about him that has hypnotized our community? Well, join us as we explore what about him has everyone captivated and how he has impacted the history of anime/manga and society going forward.

events.title: Cosplay Talent Show
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] We all know that cosplayers are talented: masters of sewing, crafting, wig-styling, modeling, and performing. But... can they make balloon animals? Play the clarinet? Tap dance?? Mime???Come celebrate your fellow Convention attendees as they show off a diverse range of their exceptional, unconventional, and secret talents. This is not a competition, just a fun supportive place to show off!

events.title: Ani-Family Feud by Anime Nation UW
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Anime Nation UW is for another round of Ani-Family Feud!! At the University of Washington, Anime Nation is THE student organization for anime/manga/game enjoyers alike. It is our honor to bring the spirit of ANUW to the convention for the second year in a row—and we're going all out once again! Come play anime-themed Family Feud with your fellow attendees for the chance to win very special prizes!

events.title: Star Ocean Ex Episodes 1-3
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] ANIME SHOWING (Subtitled)

Based on the video game series, the story follows Claude C. Kenni, a young officer aboard an advanced starship who is mysteriously transported to Expel, a medieval world of swords and sorcery. Mistaken for a legendary hero, Claude becomes entangled in a quest to save the planet from a looming catastrophe.

events.title: Appleseed
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] ANIME SHOWING (1988 OVA - Subtitlted)

After World War III devastates the Earth, the utopian city of Olympus is governed largely by genetically engineered artificial humans known as Bioroids. When terrorists plot to use a powerful weapon called the “Holy Grail” to destabilize the fragile balance between humans and Bioroids, ESWAT officers Deunan Knute and the cyborg Briareos are drawn into a high-stakes conflict that threatens the future of the city.

events.title: Taboo! Game Show: Anime Edition
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] It's the Taboo! Game Show: Anime Edition! Compete in a team of 2-4 and try to get your teammates to guess anime-related terms. Think fast, flex your otaku knowledge to its limits, and get your team as many points as possible! Be careful not to use any of the "taboo" words, or we and the audience will buzz you! events.title: Disability and Access in Witch Hat Atelier
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] In the world of magic, you are either born a witch or you aren't. This is supposedly to keep people from misusing magic to harm others, but Coco and company continue to see the inequity and suffering caused and emphasized by lack of access and judgment of ability. In this panel, we'll explore how Witch Hat Atelier's whimsical story is a clear commentary on issues we experience in our world on a daily basis, and why those who need access the most are often barred from it.

events.title: The GameSurge | Ultimate Video Game Trivia!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Put your gaming knowledge on the line in the ultimate trivia showdown! From retro classics to modern masterpieces, contestants face off for bragging rights in this high-energy, crowd-powered celebration of all things video games. Survive the rounds and earn your chance to raid The Treasure Trove of Trash—where fantastic prizes (and maybe a few surprises) await. Do YOU have what it takes to claim the loot and the glory? There’s only one way to find out!

events.title: Music from Anime: Live!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Live performance of music from some of our favorite anime; arranged for wind quintet, plus some special guest artists. There are many fantastic composers and song writers working in the realm of anime. We will be performing music by Joe Hisaishi (Studio Ghibli), Evan Call (Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End, Violet Evergarden), Yuki Kajiura (Sis Puella Magica Madoka), and more!

events.title: Yen Press Industry Panel
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] For over a decade, Yen Press has been one of the biggest publishers of manga, releasing the likes of Black Butler, Fruits Basket and Toilet-bound Hanako-kun. Yen Press has also given a home to fan favorite categories such as light novels, audiobooks and m

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. events.title: A Study of Tatsuki Fujimoto: The Man Behind Chainsaw Man
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Following the release of 2022's Chainsaw Man, and the hit 2025 Reze Arc film, Tatsuki Fujimoto has quickly made a name for himself as one of the premier creators of the manga industry. With his bold, cinematic visual style, and gripping, eccentric storytelling, it is rare to find a creator that captures the hearts of millions of fans all over the world writing seemingly whatever he wants! If you are interested in exploring the underlying themes of works such as Chainsaw Man or Fire Punch, or even just learning some funny anecdotes from this largely mysterious figure, then join in for some lively discussion!

events.panelDescription: [Gaming] I've run out of Digimon puns and lyrics, so unfortunately you'll just have to settle with a Sealed tournament for free! All wrapped in the package of some beautiful cardboard. If you want to learn a brand new TCG that is just starting this is your chance to get in on the ground floor! All participants will receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!

All our materials for Echoes of Astra were graciously donated by Parhelion Studios, so a big thank you to them for allowing us to show their game to you. Come over to the TCG Room and one of our experienced volunteers will teach you all about this tactically rich, exciting new game!

When Earth faces a looming alien threat, the outrageously wealthy and arrogant B-ko is far more concerned with defeating her rival A-ko and winning the affection of their sweet, clueless friend C-ko. Armed with custom-built mecha and an army of loyal followers, B-ko wages over-the-top battles in a fast-paced parody packed with classic anime references, exaggerated superpowers, and a stylish red power suit.

events.title: Avatar: Seven Havens - What We Know So Far
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] 20 years after the debut of Avatar: The Last Airbender, co-creators Michael Dante DiMartino and Bryan Konietzko are expanding their beloved franchise again in 2027 with another 2D animated series: Avatar: Seven Havens. Set years after The Legend of Korra, both the physical and spiritual worlds have been devastated by a cataclysm, and the Avatar is feared as humanity’s destroyer rather than its savior. The premise signals a bold new direction for the franchise. Can Avatar: Seven Havens live up to the standards of what many consider one of the greatest animated series ever, or will it face the same divisive reception as Korra, Netflix’s 2024 adaptation, and a certain 2010 film best left unnamed? Join a PNW panelist, filmmaker, and lazy cosplayer alongside fellow local fans for an interactive discussion and Q&A on what Avatar fans truly deserve—and whether we’re actually going to get it.

events.title: The Evolution of the Magical Girl Genre: 1950 - 2010
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A survey of the Magical Girl genre from it's early beginnings in the 50's, to its codification in the 90's and explosion in the 00's. We'll be talking about notable anime that shaped this genre, as well as the media ecosystem that spawns them. This is part one of two panels, the part two will cover modern day magical girl anime.

We've got the anime glasses gleam, we've got cyber glasses, we've got glasses with windshield wipers on them, we've got nerds and gentlemen, teachers and spies! The potential of glasses is truly terrifying.

[Fan Panel] Presented by CMDR Sariah and Winterblade AMVs

Have you ever seen a gut-busting AMV and wanted to make one just like it? Comedy AMVs can be some of the most memorable, diverse, and uplifting videos in any contest, and they can be either very simple or very complex! This panel will use several videos as case studies to showcase the many ways to brainstorm, edit, and fine-tune a comedy AMV. By the end, you’ll know how to squeeze every giggle out of your audience!

This is an audience participation panel, so be sure to bring your wit and romantic spirit.

events.title: Work in Japan on the JET Program: Info Session (Panel)
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Are you interested in working in Japan? The Japan Exchange Teaching (JET) Program has brought over 80,000 participants from more than 80 countries to work in schools, boards of education, and government offices throughout Japan! The JET Program is the only teaching exchange program managed by the government of Japan. Learn more about the application process and hear from former participants about their time in Japan and the impact that the JET Program has had on their lives.Note: A bachelor’s degree is a minimum requirement for this job opportunity.

events.title: Ghost in the Shell and the Stray Dog of Anime Mamoru Oshii
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] One of the most seminal anime directors to have ever lived Mamoru Oshii, has had an indelible mark on pop culture as a whole. While most famous in the United States for the 1995 Sci-Fi classic Ghost in the Shell, Oshii has created a number of other works covering ideas just as nuanced and thought provoking, with tones ranging from the melancholic to the absolutely absurd! If you are interested in exploring the underlying ideas of Ghost in the Shell, and finding other works that could possibly scratch that same itch, then join in as we discuss the career of Mamoru Oshii, a man always questioning whether our reality could really be true.

events.title: The Science of Pokémon Geography
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] From the volcanoes of Cinnabar Island to the caves of Mt. Moon, the geography of Pokémon hides a world of science waiting to be uncovered. Hosted by a geoscientist, we'll explore landscapes, biomes, and hazards that shape Pokémon habitats and cities. This panel blends science with worldbuilding to reveal the Pokémon world in a whole new way.

events.title: Moto Hagio: Reality, Gender and Identity
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Legendary writer and manga artist, Moto Hagio, called the "God of Shoujo Manga", has written some of the most influential stories across multiple genres but is scarcely known in popular media in the United States. This panel will discuss, in brief, some of the ground-breaking themes in her books and series that have contributed to and shaped many Japanese manga artists that followed after.

events.title: Is That An Isekai? You As An Attendee Can Cast A Vote!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join this panelist & co. by voting on a variety of both Eastern and Western shows/movies/books/etc. as to whether you think they count as Isekai or not! This will be a casual panel with some back-and-forth by the two panelists and a majority vote from the audience for each concept. Every time a vote is taken, the winning result will be recorded. This panel will be held at multiple other conventions in the US over the next few years in order to get data on how regional differences may affect these opinions.

events.title: Fifth Gen Isn’t Pokemon
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Generation five of pokemon introduced a lot of new concepts to the series like ripping off their previous aesthetic designs, min-maxing stats to optimize the removal of actual pokemon from competitive play, and whatever Garbador is. When it comes down to it the abominations that were introduced in Pokemon Black and White are nowhere near as majestic as classic pokemon like Muk, Mr. Mime, and Electrode.

events.title: AMV Iron Editor
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] One of the AMV Theater's most popular events! 2 Editors will go head to head in a live editing competition where they will have to make an AMV on the spot to the theme “Totally 80s!" There will be games and prizes so audience participation is a must!

events.title: Blitz Shogi Handicap Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] A fun blitz Tournament. Games are handicap so all players have a chance to win.

events.title: Pokemania: A Look Back at When Pokemon Took the World by Storm
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Move over Mickey, there's a new mouse in town! When Pokemon was first released in 1996, few people realized just how big it would become. Over the next few years, it took the world by storm and started a Pokemon obsession that would lead to the most profitable media franchise in the world. Whether you're hoping to relive those glory days or wishing to get a glimpse into a time when you weren't even born yet, join us as we look back at the Pokemon craze of 1999 in North America.

events.title: Mecha Modeling Contest
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] The contest will have prizes across five categories:

1. Best Over All
2. Custom Build
3. Advanced Build
4. Straight Build
5. People’s Choice

While we do recommend pre-registering for the contest, on-site registration will also be available until 2 pm on Saturday, with judging and the awards ceremony taking place on Saturday at 6pm.

Pre-registration can be done using the form here :

https://forms.gle/Hp2gxn8mH3soNG3ZA

To participate in the contest, you will need to bring your kit to the Mecha Modeling room anytime on Friday or Saturday before 2pm. Only one entry is allowed per person. When registering, please choose the appropriate category for your kit. We reserve the right to move your kit to a different category if we feel it is necessary. People’s Choice voting will take place during the official judging so come to our room at 6pm on Saturday if you’d like to make your voice heard.

events.title: Improvaganza
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A whole different kind of improv comedy show from the panelists who bring you Whose Line is it Anyway. With all new games to play, such as Mission Improbable, Time Warp, Film Noire, and more. Come join us for all the laughs and fun.

events.title: Cosplay Contest
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage] See your favorite characters come to life on stage!  The Sakura-Con Cosplay Contest is a competition showcase of costume construction and stage performance, open to Cosplayers of all levels of experience.

events.title: Echoes of Astra - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] This one's new! Echoes of Astra is a brand-new TCG from Parhelion Studios, an anime art themed game that mixes traditional TCG Gameplay with the tactical depth of war gaming! All wrapped in the package of some beautiful cardboard. If you want to learn a brand new TCG that is just starting this is your chance to get in on the ground floor! All participants will receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!

All our materials for Echoes of Astra were graciously donated by Parhelion Studios, so a big thank you to them for allowing us to show their game to you. Come over to the TCG Room and one of our experienced volunteers will teach you all about this tactically rich, exciting new game!

events.title: HoloLive TCG - Free Beginner Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Whether you've just learned to play or are already a fan, this beginner tournament is for you. Loaner decks will be available for this event as well, so come on over and we'll get you some cool prizes for your effort as well! And as always, all participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday.Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Netrunner -- Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come learn the Netrunner TCG directly from the team developing it! Null Signal Games has sent us their very best to teach you how to be the best Runner you can be! All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: One Piece TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Obtain Fame, Wealth, and Power beyond your wildest dreams by coming to the TCG Room and learning to play the One Piece TCG! Will you be able to find the buried treasure- Or perhaps the treasure is actually the friendships you formed in your journey? No matter what, if you complete a learn to play session you will receive a ticket for our Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: Skullgirls 2nd Encore Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The indie fighting game with AAA animation! Infinite combo protection, incredible art, and a passionate community. Win prizes!

events.title: Street Fighter III: 3rd Strike Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The parry game that defined a generation! Show Sakura-Con why 3rd Strike is still the thinking player's Street Fighter. Every match is a chance to go down in convention history. Win prizes!

events.title: FGC Trivia 2
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Round 2 of fighting game knowledge! More questions, more rounds, more chances to flex your FGC expertise. Grab your team and dominate! Win prizes!

events.title: Tekken 8 Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The King of Iron Fist Tournament continues! Heat system brings the aggression. Family drama has never been this hype. Top 3 win prizes!

events.title: How to Idol: Dance Workshop
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you interested in learning to dance like your favorite idols? Come learn tips & tricks from experienced idols & dancers! We'll be teaching a short J-pop dance - all skill levels welcome, no experience required!

events.title: Monthly Girls’ Nozaki-kun Episodes 1-4
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed)

High schooler Chiyo Sakura finally works up the courage to confess her feelings to her crush, Umetarou Nozaki—only to discover he’s a renowned shōjo manga artist who mistakes her confession for fan admiration. Instead of romance, she receives an autograph and an offer to become his assistant. Determined to get closer to him, Sakura steps into the chaotic, behind-the-scenes world of manga creation.

events.title: Q&A with Chris Hackney
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Favorite character? Memorable moments? Best places to eat in LA?! It's your time to ask Chris anything (within reason) you want and get the scoop straight from the horse's mouth! Come chill, ask your Q's, and let's learn about each other.

events.title: Why BL isn’t Just Eye Candy: Representation in ALL Flavours
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] If a panel about the wonders of BL sounds even remotely up your alley, then have we got the chance encounter for you! This is the perfect opportunity to hear about BL from an enthusiast of the genre, and why it's perfect for all kinds of readers and viewers. From manga to anime to book, there's lots to talk about with this expansive media category. This lecture-style talk about BL is perfect for the open mind, and who knows, maybe there's a thing or two to be learned!

events.title: Mecha History: Armageddon
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] In the history of mecha anime, there's not much that can't be discussed in an ordinary panel... but what's there is pretty funny! The first half of this panel will be a shortened version of the history discussed in the other panel, with a few extra details I can't cover. While the second half is all about the history of mecha in video games, which I just don't have time to cover in the other panel!

events.title: Faye Mata AMA
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Meet a useless Goddess, a pink haired boy, a sexy genius fox girl, and a tiny mischievous yordle creature with a fairy all at once! Faye Mata has voiced over 100 credited characters in video games and animated series, worked as a games industry program manager, an eSports competitor, and a wholesome content creator, and you can ask her anything!

events.title: Blue Encount Concert
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Anime so bad it's good ... the second course
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] We all know about great anime, wonderfully crafted stories, and breathtaking performances. But on the other end of the spectrum is camp! Media that is so bad, it’s good. Join us O_O ...as we serve up a brand new menu of some of the campiest and schlockiest anime.

events.title: Hear Me Out
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Is there a character from an anime or other piece of media that you’ve got the hots for, even if there’s no logical explanation for it? We want to hear your most galaxy brained, unhinged takes on which characters (or abstract concepts) you have a crush on!

events.title: JET Program Alumni Meet-Up
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] “Once you’re a JET, you’re always a JET!” The Consulate-General of Japan in Seattle is teaming up with the PNW JET Alumni Association to host the JET Alumni Meet-Up! There is life beyond the BoE, and truth can be stranger than fiction when it comes to the daily life of a JET working in Japan! We know you’re here: come meet other nerdy alumni to connect, reminisce, and share your real-life anime-zing stories! If you're not a yet a JET, come join us to learn about the “JET Experience” from those who have “been there and done that” at this relaxed, fun, and informal gathering.

events.title: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate Top 8 Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The best of the best clash in the grand finals! Watch the tournament champions crowned in the hypest Smash matches of the weekend!

events.title: Learn Para Para!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Learn a short Para Para dance routine with gyaru! Para Para is a Japanese synchronized dance style performed to Eurobeat music. It became popular among gyaru in the 90's and early 2000's! Para Para mainly involves side stepping and arm movements, so it's perfect for both very beginner dancers, and is fun for those more experienced as well!

events.title: 18+ Lip Sync Battle Doors Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Spicy Pokemon Jeopardy
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Hey guys, did you know that a Gardevoir is coming to you tonight with a brutally spicy Jeopardy game? Do iconic Pokemon like Vaporeon, Lucario, and Lopunny make you feel a certain way? In this fast-paced barrage of cultured Pokemon content, teams of enlightened adults compete against each other and the entire audience for prizes. This Python-based Jeopardy game that some random Gardevoir cosplayer coded from scratch will feature thighs, grippers, memes, and other stimulating categories. Regardless of previous experience with the Pokemon franchise, attendee participation is crucial in each of the two rounds that will be played! Come, cringe, and laugh while indulging in the chaos flowing through the screen! Warning: After this panel, you may be able to 1) identify Pokemon based on conspicuous features, 2) perceive certain Pokemon in ways you never have before, and 3) more fully enjoy your convention experience.

events.title: Multiverse Comedy Show
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] The world, nay the universe needs to laugh! Using the Improbability Drive to out-maneuver the Vogon fleet; we’ve gathered personalities from across the galaxies for a brand new show! Introducing "Multiverse Got Talent", a cosplay comedy show that unites fandoms to prove that humor brings us together. Using the Improbability drive; your host, Steve Katz, will be your guide for the evening, as you journey across the bridge of the Enterprise; or the swamps of Far Far Away; or into the TARDIS itself. No matter who pops in, you will leave wanting more. Remember, Don’t Panic… cause its time to laugh! (Simply put: comedians in cosplay performing in character; and it’s a ton of fun!)

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: 18+ Lip Sync Battle
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Unleash your inner superstar in this high-energy lip sync battle for adults only! Whether you’re channeling your favorite diva, rocking out to a power ballad, or bringing the heat with a sultry performance, this is your chance to take the stage and captivate the crowd. With no limits on creativity (or a bit of sass), participants will battle it out to claim the title of Best In Show in this electrifying 18+ Lip Sync Battle.

events.title: Anime Survivor
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Anime Survivor takes your favorite anime characters and strands them on an island where they have to compete until only one is left. We utilize a web-based simulator to find out once and for all which anime character has what it takes to be the sole survivor.

events.title: Cosplay Figure Drawing
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Need inspiration for your next piece? Want to reference your favorite character irl? Just want to practice fun poses? Come to our figure drawing studio! The sessions will consist of several rounds of incrementally timed sets with different poses and models in cosplay. Come with your art tools of choice, or pencils and paper will be provided We will have models of different body types and from multiple fandoms. All models will be clothed in accordance with Sakuracon dress code and policies.

events.title: Flesh and Blood - Free Draft Night Party!
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Swing big for a knockout as cheers and boos amp up the volume in this Super Slam booster draft! Build a deck on the fly as a face (Revered) or heel (Reviled) to play in a limited environment that will 100% surprise you with its technical depth and intricacies. Brownie points if you watched that one SuperEyePatch Wolf video that uses anime to explain pro wrestling! Also we respect Brody King in this house.  All participants also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

BONUS: If you see a bald guy in a power-chair (Or just ask for Stein) and tell him your favorite wrestler, he'll give you an extra promo card! 

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

Whether you’re new to AMVs or a seasoned connoisseur, we will help you find the right videos for any occasion. Come and enjoy our array of choices as we present flight after flight of AMVs to satisfy any taste — all hand-picked and free of additives and pretentiousness!

events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Test your yuri prowess in this team based Jeopardy-style trivia game! Featuring both popular and niche titles with a focus on manga, yuri fans will be put to the test in multiple rounds of varied categories. Players of all knowledge are welcome!

events.title: Cute or Criminal: Romance? In Anime
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Whoop whoop! That's the sound of the police! They're coming for you because there are some things in anime that are just down right illegal. Join us for a spin off of our long time panel, Cute or Creepy: Romance in Anime, where we will explore the more mature side of romance in anime. This is an 18+ panel that relies heavily on audience participation, so be sure to bring your wit and old-timey detective hat, as we decide whether clips are cute or criminal.

events.title: Anisong Trivia: Hard Mode
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you an anime song super fan? Do you have esoteric anime knowledge many others don't? Then this is the panel is for you! With anime songs ranging from the mainstream to the obscure, this panel is geared towards the most seasoned anime music fans. Competitors will be seeded in a live qualifier and placed will be placed into rounds where competitors pick stylized categories similar to Jeopardy. The best players will be placed into a grand final to determine the champion!

Come find out and learn Grand Archive, a growing new TCG fusing anime aesthetics with the best of western game design! Along with your demo kit, all participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

All material graciously provided by Grand Archive’s developer Weebs of the Shore. Thank you for your continued support, WotS!

events.title: Zapp's Spaceship of Love: Great Big PERV Debate!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Yaoi vs Yuri, which is the purest form of love? Cowgirl's vs Cow Girls, how much can we milk this joke? Was it difficult to get through this description, or was it not hard enough? Don't worry, it happens to lots of guys... Come watch our degenerates debate the perverse, the lascivious, and the highly inappropriate, in this special 18+ adult only extra spicy edition of the Great Big Nerd Debate! Who knows, we might even let YOU argue on stage!

events.title: Is It Wrong to Try to Pick Up Girls in a Dungeon?  Episodes 1  - 4
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed)

In the bustling city of Orario, where gods and goddesses live among mortals and sponsor adventuring “familias,” 14-year-old Bell Cranel is the sole member of the Hestia Familia. Eager to prove himself in the deadly dungeon beneath the city, Bell’s life changes after a fateful encounter with the powerful “Sword Princess,” Ais Wallenstein—an encounter she’s unlikely to forget.

events.title: The Midnight Run: Toonami Tribute
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Ride aboard the Absolution Mk. XIV as TOM and SARA host a late night AMV tribute to our favorite Toonami shows, both old and new. Stay gold!

events.title: Overnight TCG Freeplay - Saturday Night
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Did you just get out of the Rave, or a late-night panel, perhaps? Are you looking to still have some fun at the Con overnight? Well, if you brought your own cards, we've got space! The TCG Room may close its outer doors at midnight, but we have a number of free-play tables available for BYO style play. Want to jam out some 2 AM Commander? We got you.

This area is not staffed between the hours of Midnight and 9 AM. All tables are available for use, but we ask that you please clean up after yourselves and behave responsibly in the game space. Failure to comply with this is a violation of intergalactic law. Thank you, and see you bright and early for more scheduled content!

events.title: DRAW PHASE Presents TCGs @ Sakura-Con - Sunday
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Welcome to the Sakura-Con x DRAW PHASE 2026 Trading Card Games room! We'll be running totally FREE events wall to wall all weekend long, from Learn to Play events, to Drafts, Sealed, Constructed, and more for some of your favorite games! Any event you do in the room will earn you a ticket that you can jealously hoard until Sunday when we have our Grand Giveaway, full of prizes generously donated by our lovely sponsors, donors, and friends. 

Whether you're a seasoned pro or a you've never played a card game in your life, we'll have content for you! Come learn a game you have always wanted to play or one you've never even heard of. And best of all, every single thing in this room is FREE! So if you want to come hang out with your fellow con-goer, meet some new friends and learn some fun games along the way, our team of dedicated volunteers will be here all weekend to guide you through the wonderful world of cardboard.

DRAW PHASE is a 501(c)(3) Non-Profit Organization that provides TCG Content in the PNW at no cost to the attendee! If you're interested in learning more, ask a volunteer.

events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Dear Con-goer, do you desire Flesh? Do you desire Blood? Perhaps even both at the same time? In that case, we at the TCG Room have you covered. Come see us and one of our Flesh Experts (Blood Experts available upon request) will teach you all the ins and outs of the Flesh and Blood TCG. Should you survive the trials, you will be rewarded with a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

All materials for Flesh and Blood were graciously donated by Legend Story Studios, who are a fantastic partner.

events.title: AMV 201: Advanced Techniques
events.panelDescription:
[Fan Panel] Presented by CMDR Sariah and Winterblade AMVs

Take your AMVs to the next level! If After Effects plugins leave you puzzled, you're struggling to break into finals, or you just want some cool new techniques to add to your toolkit, then you've come to the right place. We'll answer your questions and help troubleshoot common problems. We'll show off the tools and processes that have helped us the most. Whether you're trying to impress a YouTube audience or a Sakura-Con judge, this panel is a must for all aspiring AMV editors!

events.title: Grand Archive – Free Constructed Tournament
events.panelDescription:

events.title: Kyoto Animation Arson Attack - 7th Year Memorial Service
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] In July 2019, the studio and publisher Kyoto Animation experienced a horrific and devastating arson attack that resulted in the deaths of 36 young animators, making it one of Japan’s worst massacres in recent history. Because the 1st, 3rd, and 7th year memorial services are of particular importance in Japanese tradition, we feel that the people who lost their lives that day were not given the appropriate opportunity to be mourned by their fans due to the interruption caused by the COVID 19 pandemic.  We, as a local Buddhist temple that is a direct descendant of the 1,200 year old Japanese Shingon lineage, believe that being able to provide fans the opportunity to participate in a 7th year memorial service would be particularly significant. We feel that it is our obligation to provide the opportunity for people to grieve and mourn the lives of the victims and honor their memories. Through the Buddhist memorial service, we will be able to convey to the spirits of the deceased that their lives, works, and legacies are meaningful, and will be cherished. This will also help mourners attain the understanding that their lives are one with the victims of the attack. When a work of art moves someone, it becomes an inseparable part of them, and so we carry its legacy on with us through our own lives. In this way, we are all on a shared voyage, helping those people who have positively impacted our lives continue to live on through us, and we hope to allow fans of the works made by the fallen animators to experience a deeper connection with this understanding.
